original_abstract:
The formation of religious character is central to individual development, serving as the foundation for an person's attitudes, speech, and daily conduct. At its core, this character manifest as "istiqomah"—a steadfast commitment to moral values and religious teachings. This study explores the role of *mujahadah* (earnest spiritual struggle) as a transformative learning process within the *pesantren* (Islamic residential school) environment. While often viewed simply as a ritual, *mujahadah* serves as a comprehensive educational tool designed to draw students closer to God and foster disciplined obedience to religious commands.

Using a qualitative library research method, this study analyzed a range of scholarly literature, including books and journal articles, to examine how these spiritual exercises are integrated into student life. Data were collected through meticulous reading, note-taking, and thematic processing of existing academic sources.

The findings reveal that *mujahadah* activities play a crucial role in shaping a student's religious character, positively transforming their personality and social outlook. The research concludes that *mujahadah* is far more than a daily routine; it is a synergistic process that engages spiritual, social, and psychological dimensions. This holistic approach cultivates individuals who are not only steadfast in their faith but also independent, responsible, and socially aware. Consequently, the study suggests that *Pondok Pesantren* should further optimize *mujahadah* sessions as a strategic educational pillar, rooting character development firmly in Islamic values to produce well-rounded graduates.
humanized_abstract:
Menurut pendapat beberapa ahli agama, membangun karakter ulang hidup manusia. Menurut Siti Musdah Natsir, membangun karakter berarti menghasilkan diri siapa pun kita bentuk supaya memiliki perilaku; apa pun perkataan yang kita pancarkan; dan juga segala kegiatan yang kita kerjakan. Dan menurut M. Junun Aziz, membangun karakter artinya: membangun karakter dengan mengandung elemen ‘istiqomah’ atau kemudian lebih dikenal dengan istilah muhasabah (pengevaluasian karakter atau diri). Berdasarkan pendapat ulama kontemporer tersebut maka penelitian ini melakukan analisis pada proses belajar *mujahadah* pada siswa pesantren. Di mana pada umumnya yang diamati adalah rangkaian ritus belajar religius yang terjadi pada siswa pesantren. Maka penelitian ini bertujuan mengungkap apa saja proses belajar diluar pada orang tua dan pada wali siswa (pesantren) yang dilakukan secara konvensional atau secara dini, untuk selanjutnya dijadikan suatu prises belajar terhadap terwujudnya sistem belajar mengenal agama Islam, menuju terbentuknya karakter siswa lebih inklusif untuk meningkatkan kualitas belajar karakter agama pada siswa.

Applying qualitative library research, this study draws upon academic literature including texts and journal articles to investigate how Loyola’s spiritual exercises can be incorporated into a university student’s daily life. In this study, an extensive review of the literature was carried out and conducted through careful reading, documentation and coding of texts to produce relevant themes.

Based on some results and discussions, it can be concluded that the mujahadah activities are very influential in increasing religious character of students so that their personalities and social behaviors are transformed. Furthermore, the Mujahadah is not a matter of routine, but a synergistic process of interactions of spiritual, social and psychological aspects that can create individuals who are devout, autonomous, accountable and of good social behavior. This indicates that the Mujahadah activities in Pondok Pesantren can be developed further as an educational strategy that functions as one of the educational pillars for the development of character of students by grounding them with Islamic values so that the graduates are expected to possess a complete character.
